title: Alternation in regular expressions should avoid containing empty alternatives
description: Regular expressions with empty alternatives can cause unexpected behavior by matching any input. Empty alternatives occur when there are consecutive pipe characters (||) or when a pipe appears at the start (|pattern) or end (pattern|) of an alternation group, creating an empty alternative that matches everything.
recommendation: Remove empty alternatives from regular expressions. Replace patterns like /foo||bar/ with /foo|bar/, and /pattern|/ with /pattern/.
impact: Unexpected regex matching behavior and potential bugs

checks.message: Remove empty alternatives from this regular expression. Empty alternatives (like ||, |$, or ^|) cause the regex to match any input unexpectedly.
